data.narrative:
Precision Neuroscience is a Series C-stage biotech company that has raised $180M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private—operating in Biotech & Health across overlapping or adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage advancement to the $180M raised proxy, implying $306M. Peer-median analysis examines four same-sector comparables spanning Series B and Series C: Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B), Chai Discovery ($3.8B), New Limit ($3.1B), and Eikon Therapeutics ($539M). The median across this cohort is $3.5B, with a full range of $539M to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data on file. The triangulated range across applicable methods spans $260M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B. Key uncertainties include stage-specific revenue trajectory, competitive differentiation within the crowded biotech sector, and execution risk through Series D. The wide peer range ($539M–$10.3B) reflects high dispersion in frontier-tech valuations at this stage.
